title: aloe divaricata subsp. tulearensis
intro: Aloe divaricata subsp. tulearensis, also known as Madagascar aloes, is a subspecies of the aloe plant native to the island of Madagascar. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Aloe divaricata subsp. tulearensis include treating skin conditions such as wounds, burns, and eczema, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into a gel or juice, which can be applied topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
